name: one arm dip
instructions: Stand facing away from a bench or chair, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place one hand on the bench or chair behind you, fingers pointing towards your body.
Extend your legs out in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench or chair.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.

Stand facing away from a bench or chair, with your feet shoulder-width apart. Place one hand on the bench or chair behind you, fingers pointing towards your body. Extend your legs out in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground. Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench or chair. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
